title: NCC Visual Canon Prompt Attempt Record
description: One JSONL object represents one canonical prompt attempt. Exactly one registry record per canonical prompt_id.

prompt_id.description: Canonical prompt ID. Format: <CHARACTER_ID>_TEST<NN>_<SCENE_ID_UPPER>_V<VERSION>. Variant label is separate.

character_ids.description: All characters involved in the attempt. First entry is the primary character.

primary_character_id.description: Redundant but stable primary character identifier. Must appear in character_ids.

test_id.description: Required for control tests and scene tests.

test_number.description: Integer test number. Required for control tests.

version.description: Attempt version, e.g. V1, V2, V4.

variant_label.description: Optional descriptive label. Never part of canonical prompt_id.

backend.description: Image generation backend or pipeline used.

prompt_source.description: Path to working-prompt section, or marker such as exact_user_visible_prompt / reconstructed_from_conversation_and_approved_result / unknown_requires_manual_input.

prompt_heading.description: Markdown heading anchor inside the prompt source file.

reference_paths.description: Repository or local paths used as references for this attempt.

output_path.description: Path to the selected output. Conditionally required.

verdict.description: Machine attempt verdict. Legacy detailed verdicts may be mapped during validation.

selected.description: True if this version was selected for deployment.

role.description: Role of the selected output. Required when selected is true.

storage.description: Storage tier. Required for selected outputs.

content_tier.description: Content tier. Required for selected outputs.

supersedes.description: Prompt IDs this selected version supersedes.

human_approval.description: True / identifier when a human approved this record.

legacy_compatibility.description: Historical records may contain additional fields (e.g. 'character' instead of 'character_ids') or legacy verdict strings. Validators must support a compatibility mode that warns but does not fail for these records. Migration to strict mode is explicit and character-by-character.
